forecast.name: This Afternoon
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain before 3pm, then rain and patchy fog. Cloudy, with a high near 46. South wind around 12 mph, with gusts as high as 23 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain and patchy fog before 4am, then a chance of sleet and rain and snow. Cloudy, with a low around 34. Northwest wind 7 to 13 mph. Chance of precipitation is 100%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.

weather_report:
In Boston today, the lingering embrace of rain and gloom settles upon the city, cloaked in a shroud of clouds that seem to weigh heavily on the horizon. The expected precipitation adds a soft whisper of melancholy to the afternoon, with the air thick and heavy, and visibility muddied by patches of fog that gently coat the streets and soften the edges of the skyline. As the day drifts towards evening, temperatures will dip into a cool embrace, signaling an impending mix of sleet and snow that dances on the cusp of winter’s lingering presence, holding court in a world more gray than bright.

Amid such dampness, warmth becomes paramount; woolen layers and waterproof jackets are your allies as you embark on your errands or that long-awaited bookshop visit. Perhaps a cozy corner café, the steam from your drink rising against the chill, serves as the perfect refuge to needle your thoughts through pages of an old favorite. The weather invites contemplation, a gentle pause in the bustle, where you might linger a moment longer, savoring the richness of a rainy Boston afternoon.
